title: Hearing Loss in the Newborn Infant

blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ Hearing Loss in the Newborn Infant?
Hearing loss (HL) เป็น most common birth defect diagnosed via newborn screening — 1–3/1,000 live births; แต่เพิ่มเป็น 3–5/1,000 เมื่อถึงวัยเรียน เพราะ late-onset HL ที่ newborn screen พลาดได้. (1) NICU infants เสี่ยงสูงกว่า well-baby nursery เป็นพิเศษ: extreme prematurity, ECMO, ototoxic drugs, hyperbilirubinemia, CMV — risk factors หลายข้อพร้อมกัน; (2) NICU infants ต้องใช้ AABR เท่านั้น — ไม่ใช้ OAE เพราะเสี่ยง auditory neuropathy ซึ่ง OAE จะ pass ทั้งที่มี HL จริง; (3) Early intervention ก่อน 6 เดือน → language scores เทียบเท่า hearing peers (Yoshinaga-Itano Ment Retard Dev Disabil Res Rev 2003); (4) HL ที่ไม่ได้รับการ diagnose → communication delay, academic failure, social-emotional impairment — สิ่งที่ป้องกันได้ถ้า screen ทัน; (5) 50% HL เป็น hereditary — GJB2 (connexin 26) เป็น single gene ที่พบบ่อยที่สุด. เรารู้เรื่องนี้เพื่อ screen ถูกวิธี, refer ถูกเวลา, และ protect hearing ใน NICU.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
อธิบาย anatomy ของ auditory pathway ตั้งแต่ outer ear ถึง auditory cortex และกลไกที่ทำให้เกิด sensorineural, conductive, neural, transient conductive HL
ระบุ degrees ของ HL: slight (16–25 dB), mild (26–40), moderate (41–55), moderately severe (56–70), severe (71–90), profound (≥91 dB)
เปรียบเทียบ OAE vs AABR vs diagnostic ABR: mechanism, HL type ที่ detect ได้, ข้อจำกัด และ เหตุผลที่ NICU ต้องใช้ AABR เท่านั้น
อธิบาย 1-3-6 rule: screen ≤1 month, diagnostic ≤3 months, intervention ≤6 months — และ evidence ที่รองรับจาก Colorado Newborn Hearing Screening Project
ระบุ etiology ของ HL: 50% hereditary; GJB2 connexin 26 = ≤50% nonsyndromic profound HL; 35delG mutation = ≤70% ของ GJB2 mutations; syndromes ที่สำคัญ 5 ชนิด
ท่องจำ JCIH 2019 risk factors สำหรับ congenital/delayed-onset HL ทั้ง 11 ข้อ (Box 61.1) โดยเน้น NICU-specific risk factors
วางแผน medical workup หลัง confirm HL: otolaryngologist, genetics evaluation, ophthalmology, developmental assessment — เหตุผลที่ต้องรอบด้าน
อธิบาย otitis media with effusion (OME) ในเด็กที่มี permanent HL: ผลต่อ hearing, management, timing ของ tympanostomy
เปรียบเทียบ 5 communication options และ audiologic devices: hearing aids (ITE/BTE), FM systems, cochlear implants — candidacy age 9 months (2020), meningitis risk
อธิบาย mitochondrial 12S rRNA mutation + aminoglycoside → ototoxicity: prevalence ใน NICU, clinical implication
อธิบาย CMV กับ Zika ใน context ของ HL: late-onset HL, screening method ที่ต้องเลือก, valganciclovir controversy
อธิบาย family stress continuum: screen fail → rescreen fail → diagnosis → intervention — strategies ที่ physician ช่วยได้
1. Anatomy ของ Auditory Pathway และ Types of Hearing Loss
1.1 Anatomy — จาก Sound Wave ถึง Auditory Cortex
Outer ear: pinna → outer ear canal → tympanic membrane; vibrations enter middle ear
Middle ear: ossicles (malleus, incus, stapes) amplify + transmit vibrations → fluid within cochlea (inner ear)

Inner ear (cochlea): sound waves in fluid → stimulate outer hair cells (OHC) + inner hair cells (IHC); OHC produce otoacoustic emissions (OAE) = echo; IHC convert mechanical → electrical energy → cochlear branch of CN VIII → brainstem → auditory cortex
Auditory neuropathy (neural HL): pathology of IHC + CN VIII — outer hair cells intact → OAE passes, ABR fails; NICU infants at highest risk for this subtype
1.2 Classification of Hearing Loss

rows: Pathology CN VIII + OHC + IHC of cochlea → impairs neuroconduction
Most common permanent HL in newborns
Anatomic obstruction outer ear (atresia) or middle ear (fused ossicles)
Myelinated fibers CN VIII or IHC pathology; OHC intact
OAE passes, AABR fails; NICU infants at highest risk
Debris in canal or fluid in middle ear (OME)
Common in NICU; temporary if resolved
Combination of permanent HL + transient conductive HL

blocks.text: 1.3 Degrees of Hearing Loss (ASHA 2017)

rows: Hears only loud or amplified speech
May not hear speech at all; cochlear implant candidate

blocks.text:
Clinical Caveat — Even Slight/Mild HL Affects Language Development
Even slight HL (16–25 dB) สามารถ affect language development และอาจ progressive. สำหรับ bilateral HL, severity graded by better-functioning ear. Tomblin et al Ear Hear 2015: children with mild-severe HL — language outcomes significantly below hearing peers even with amplification. เหตุผลที่ต้องรักษา mild HL อย่างจริงจัง — ไม่ใช่แค่ severe/profound.
2. Screening และ Diagnostic Tests
2.1 OAE vs AABR vs Diagnostic ABR

rows: Microphone in canal records OHC echo-like responses to sound stimulus; automated pass/fail
Cannot detect neural HL (auditory neuropathy) — OHC intact → OAE passes even with significant neural HL; NOT for NICU
Surface electrodes record neural activity (CN VIII + brainstem) in response to click; algorithm detects wave 5 → automated pass/fail
Longer screening time vs OAE; requires quiet/asleep infant; detects sensorineural + conductive + NEURAL HL
Full ABR waveform analysis by audiologist; threshold across frequencies; cross-checks tympanometry
Requires sedation in older infants; gold standard for HL type and degree
Probe in canal; air pressure change → tympanic membrane movement; type A = normal; flat = fluid/perforation
Not used for screening; 1000 Hz probe for infants <6 months

blocks.text:
NICU Rule — ใช้ AABR เท่านั้น ห้ามใช้ OAE ใน NICU
JCIH 2007 (AAP Pediatrics 2007;120:898): NICU infants cared for >5 days ต้องใช้ AABR เท่านั้น เพราะเสี่ยงสูงสุดต่อ neural HL (auditory neuropathy/dyssynchrony) ซึ่ง OAE ไม่สามารถ detect ได้ (OHC intact → OAE passes แม้จะมี HL จริง). Some hospitals use two-step: AABR + OAE — ใน well-baby nursery acceptable; in NICU: AABR only. False-positive fail ใน OAE เพิ่มจาก: debris ในช่อง ear, OME, background noise. NICU false-positive rate higher than well-baby nursery — ต้องแยก transient conductive HL จาก permanent HL.
2.2 The 1-3-6 Rule — JCIH Benchmark
1 month: all infants screened for HL — NICU before discharge; well-baby nursery no later than 1 month
3 months: infants who do not pass → comprehensive evaluation by audiologist; confirm HL status + type; never send NICU fail to medical home — refer to audiologist directly
6 months: confirmed HL → early intervention services no later than 6 months; professionals with expertise in HL + deafness in infants
Colorado data (Yoshinaga-Itano 2003): intervention ≤6 months → speaking/sign/total communication language scores comparable to hearing children at age 3
Vohr et al Pediatrics 2008;122:535: enrollment ≤3 months → significantly ↑ words understood, words produced, gestures at 12–16 months vs enrollment >3 months
Prevalence note: screen identifies 1.79/1,000 at birth; school-age deafness 3.65/1,000 (Watkin & Baldwin Int J Audiol 2012) → rate nearly doubles by school age from late-onset/progressive HL
2.3 Behavioral Tests (สำหรับ Follow-up)
Vision Reinforcement Audiometry (VRA): developmental age ≥6 months; infant turns to sounds paired with animated toys in sound booth
Conditioned audiometry: ≥2.5 years; child places block in box when hearing sound
Standard audiometry: ≥4.5 years; behavioral response to sounds; gold standard at school age
3. Etiology ของ Hearing Loss
3.1 Genetic HL — Overview
≥50% ของ congenital HL เป็น hereditary; nearly 400 syndromes + hundreds of genes identified
Genetic HL composition: ~30% syndromic, ~70% nonsyndromic
Nonsyndromic HL genetics: 75–85% autosomal recessive (DFNB); 15–24% autosomal dominant (DFNA); 1–2% X-linked (DFN)
→ Most infants with HL are born to hearing parents (AR nonsyndromic = majority)

GJB2 (Connexin 26) — Single Most Common Gene for Hearing Loss
GJB2 encodes connexin 26, a gap-junction protein in cochlear connective tissue. Accounts for ≤50% of all profound nonsyndromic HL. >100 mutations identified. 35delG single mutation = ≤70% of all GJB2 mutations. Inheritance: autosomal recessive. Mitochondrial 12S rRNA gene mutations: associated with aminoglycoside-induced nonsyndromic HL. Prevalence in NICU populations: 1–1.8% (Ealy et al Laryngoscope 2011; Johnson et al Otolaryngol HNS 2010). Mitochondrial HL: variable age of onset — may not manifest in newborn period. Clinical implication: aminoglycosides ยังเป็น first-line ใน NICU — awareness of genetic susceptibility สำคัญสำหรับ ototoxicity monitoring.
3.2 Syndromes Associated with Hearing Loss

rows: AD; most common AD syndrome (1–4% HL)
Sensorineural HL; heterochromia iridis; white forelock; dystopia canthorum
Severe-profound HL; euthyroid goiter (adolescence+); Mondini dysplasia / dilated vestibular aqueduct on CT
Most common AR syndromic HL; vestibular problems + progressive retinitis pigmentosa → blindness; implant EARLY (vision will be lost)
Prolonged QT on ECG → sudden death + syncope; bilateral profound HL; cardiology consult for QT management
Preauricular pits, malformed pinnae, branchial fistulas, renal anomalies

blocks.text: 3.3 Risk Factors — JCIH 2019 Box 61.1

11 Risk Factors สำหรับ Permanent Congenital, Delayed-Onset, หรือ Progressive HL
1. Caregiver concern* regarding hearing/speech/language/developmental delay. 2. Family history of permanent HL*. 3. NICU >5 days + hyperbilirubinemia requiring exchange transfusion (regardless of LOS). 4. In utero infections*: CMV*, Zika*, herpes, rubella, syphilis, toxoplasmosis. 5. Craniofacial anomalies: atresia, microtia, temporal bone anomalies. 6. Physical findings associated with syndromes (e.g., white forelock → Waardenburg). 7. Syndromes*: neurofibromatosis, osteopetrosis, Usher, Waardenburg, Alport, Pendred, Jervell-Lange-Nielsen. 8. Neurodegenerative disorders*: Hunter syndrome, Friedreich ataxia, Charcot-Marie-Tooth. 9. Culture-positive postnatal infections*: confirmed bacterial/viral meningitis (especially HSV, VZV). 10. Head trauma (basal skull / temporal bone fracture requiring hospitalization). 11. Chemotherapy*. (* = greater risk for delayed-onset or progressive HL)
3.4 NICU-Specific Risk Factor Detail

Risk factor 3 — NICU exposures: ECMO, assisted ventilation, ototoxic medications (gentamicin, tobramycin), loop diuretics (furosemide), hyperbilirubinemia requiring exchange transfusion (regardless of LOS)

CMV: most common medical cause of both early- and delayed-onset HL; most infants with congenital CMV have NO clinical findings at birth (Boppana et al J Pediatr 2005; Fowler & Boppana J Clin Virol 2006). Valganciclovir for CMV without findings/HL: controversial — ↑ side effects; Kimberlin et al NEJM 2015;372:933: benefit in symptomatic congenital CMV
Zika virus: neurotropic; associated with CNS abnormalities → HL type: sensorineural or auditory neuropathy → must screen with AABR (Leal et al MMWR 2016)
Medical complications: account for 40% of childhood permanent HL; meningitis → ↑ sensorineural HL (Adachi et al Ann Otol 2010) — if post-meningitis, implant early (cochlear ossification can occur)
4. Medical Workup และ Care Coordination
4.1 Post-Diagnosis Team

Primary care physician: initial visit when screen fails (inform parents, reinforce importance of follow-up); second visit after diagnosis (review audiologist report, community resources, family support)

Otolaryngologist: comprehensive assessment for etiology; candidacy for amplification + assistive devices + surgical intervention (reconstruction, BAHA, cochlear implant); all confirmed HL must see ENT

Geneticist: ALL families with confirmed HL should be offered genetics evaluation; etiology, prognosis, associated disorders, recurrence risk; GJB2/GJB6 testing; panel for non-syndromic HL; karyotype/chromosomal if indicated
Ophthalmologist: ≥1 visual acuity exam; association of HL with vision impairment (especially Usher syndrome → progressive blindness — choose communication that is NOT vision-dependent)
Comorbidities: 30–40% of children with confirmed HL have comorbidities/other disabilities; monitor developmental milestones closely; initiate early referrals
Early intervention provider: educators of the deaf, speech-language pathologists, audiologists — 12 best-practice goals in JCIH 2013 Supplement (Table 61.4)
4.2 Middle Ear Disease (OME) in Children with HL
OME prevalence: ~90% of children have ≥1 episode before school age; 40–50% asymptomatic
Impact on permanent HL: superimposed OME → mixed HL → further compromise hearing → monitor closely in children with any permanent HL

Management: pneumatic otoscope exam; document laterality + duration + symptoms; tympanometry for middle ear function; 3-month observation first (75–90% spontaneous resolution); NO antihistamines or decongestants (no evidence of benefit)

Tympanostomy tube indication: persistent OME ≥4 months + persistent HL OR structural injury to tympanic membrane/middle ear — Rosenfeld et al Otolaryngol HNS 2013: clinical practice guideline; tubes → ↓ effusion + ↑ hearing
5. Communication Options และ Audiologic Devices
5.1 5 Communication Options

rows: Residual hearing + amplification + visual support (speech reading); goal = spoken language
Listening skills alone (no lip reading); goal = spoken language; high demand for child + family
Visual system: 8 hand shapes × 4 placements near face; supports spoken language development
All means simultaneously (speech + sign); flexible; most common early approach
Deaf children learn ASL first; English as second language; full language access for profoundly deaf

blocks.text: การเลือก communication option เปลี่ยนได้ตามอายุ + degree HL + cochlear implant status
Usher syndrome: ห้ามเลือก visual-only communication — progressive retinitis pigmentosa → blindness; ASL alone ไม่เหมาะ เพราะจะสูญเสียการมองเห็นในอนาคต; prefer auditory verbal + cochlear implant early
5.2 Hearing Aids
Types: in-the-ear (ITE) หรือ behind-the-ear (BTE); สามารถ fit ทารกได้ตั้งแต่เดือนแรกของชีวิต
Audiologist programs gain (loudness) by frequency — adjust for child's specific HL pattern; normal speech: 500–2000 Hz
Ear molds: made from ear impression; infants grow fast → may need replacement every 6–8 weeks
5.3 Frequency-Modulated (FM) Systems
For individuals with HL ในสภาพแวดล้อมที่มีเสียงรบกวน (noisy environments)
Teacher/parent wears FM transmitter + microphone → low-power radio signal → FM receiver worn by child (within 50 feet)
FM receiver → sends to personal hearing aid or cochlear implant → speech sounds like inches away; used at home, shopping, school
5.4 Cochlear Implants

Cochlear Implants — Candidacy Age ลดลงเรื่อยๆ และ Earlier = Better
Worldwide: ~736,000 recipients (as of Dec 2019 FDA data); US: ~118,000 adults + ~65,000 children. Candidacy age timeline: 2 years (1990) → 18 months (1998) → 12 months (2000) → 9 months (2020). Bilateral profound HL implanted before 12 months → best speech and language outcomes (Leigh et al Otol Neurotol 2013; Mitchell et al Laryngoscope 2020; Gagnon et al J Speech Lang Hear Res 2021). Children up to 7 years derive greatest benefit for speech development. Deafness from meningitis: implant early in first year (cochlear ossification risk). Meningitis risk post-implant: Streptococcus pneumoniae most common pathogen (Reefhuis et al NEJM 2003;349:435). → Vaccinate ALL cochlear implant recipients per AAP high-risk schedule. Monitor all patients for middle ear and other infections.
6. Continued Surveillance และ Family Support
6.1 Ongoing Surveillance in Medical Home
JCIH 2007 + 2019 recommendations: regular surveillance of developmental milestones, auditory skills, parental concerns, middle ear status — consistent with AAP periodicity schedule
Global development screens: at 9, 18, 24–30 months with validated tools for all infants
Language screening tools: Early Language Milestone Scale (ELMS); MacArthur Communicative Development Inventory (CDI); Language Development Survey (LDS); Ages and Stages Questionnaires (ASQ)
Why HL rate doubles by school age: (1) mild HL missed by newborn screen; (2) delayed-onset/progressive HL (CMV); (3) late-onset HL from meningitis/trauma/chemo
Any concern → refer to audiology (not repeat screen at home); NICU infants who fail should NEVER be rescreened in medical home — directly refer to audiologist
6.2 Family Stress and Support
Stress continuum: screen fail → rescreen fail → diagnostic fail → intervention — each step ↑ stress level
95% ของ children with congenital HL are born to hearing parents — fear of disability + unfamiliarity with deaf culture → ↑ stress
Culturally deaf parents: may have anticipated diagnosis; comfortable with HL; stress profile very different

Well-baby nursery data (Vohr et al Arch Pediatr Adolesc Med 2008): false-positive screen → ↑ worry 2–8 weeks until rescreen; informed mothers → ↓ worry; false-positive → no ↑ stress at 12–16 or 18–24 months
Protective factor: greater family resources → ↓ persistent stress; NICU stay → ↑ prolonged stress
Financial support: Part C Early Intervention case manager; hearing aid loaner program; Social Security benefit; Katie Beckett Program; Medicaid eligibility
Support groups: Hands and Voices, Family Voices; families benefit from meeting other families with D/HH children
7. NICU Practical — Hearing Screening ใน High-Risk Infants
7.1 NICU Hearing Screening Protocol
Timing: before NICU discharge (JCIH) — increasing NICUs complete full diagnostic ABR prior to discharge เพื่อ avoid delay in diagnosis ใน most medically high-risk infants
Method: AABR only — never OAE alone in NICU (misses auditory neuropathy)
Two-step screen (AABR + OAE): acceptable in well-baby nursery; not recommended in NICU
False-positive in NICU: higher rate than well-baby nursery (OME, debris); document and arrange follow-up audiologist within 3 months
7.2 Ototoxicity Monitoring in NICU
Aminoglycosides (gentamicin, tobramycin): most common NICU medications with ototoxic risk; especially dangerous in infants with mitochondrial 12S rRNA mutations (1–1.8% of NICU population)
Loop diuretics (furosemide): JCIH risk factor regardless of LOS when combined with assisted ventilation/extreme prematurity

Practical: (1) document cumulative aminoglycoside exposure; (2) TDM (trough monitoring); (3) single-daily dosing where evidence supports; (4) if prolonged exposure → schedule AABR before discharge; (5) CMV screening at birth if any NICU risk factor
7.3 Congenital CMV — Hearing Implications
CMV = most common medical cause of both early AND delayed-onset HL — most congenital CMV infants asymptomatic at birth

Valganciclovir for symptomatic congenital CMV: Kimberlin et al NEJM 2015;372:933 — 6 months oral valganciclovir ↑ hearing + language outcomes in symptomatic infants vs 6-week course; controversial for asymptomatic CMV without HL — ↑ side effects (neutropenia); decision weighs benefit vs risk

CMV + HL → late-onset: ongoing surveillance beyond newborn screen critical; repeat audiologic assessment by 24–30 months even if passes initial screen; Rawlinson et al J Clin Virol 2018: neonates with congenital CMV identified via UNHS — high rate of HL at later ages
7.4 Delivery Room / Immediate Postnatal Actions
Document any craniofacial anomalies (pinna, ear canal, preauricular pits, temporal bone) → high-risk for HL; refer to ENT + audiology
Family history of HL noted at birth → ongoing surveillance + audiologic assessment by 24–30 months even if passes screen
Post-meningitis: refer for audiologic evaluation; if HL confirmed → cochlear implant evaluation early (ossification risk within months)
Cochlear implant recipients: ensure vaccination per AAP high-risk pneumococcal schedule; monitor all middle ear + systemic infections
8. 5 Bedside Pearls

PEARL 1 — NICU: AABR เท่านั้น เพราะ Auditory Neuropathy
NICU infants >5 days มีความเสี่ยงสูงต่อ neural HL (auditory neuropathy / auditory dyssynchrony) ซึ่ง outer hair cells ยังทำงานปกติ → OAE ผ่าน ทั้งที่มี HL จริง. AABR ตรวจ CN VIII + brainstem electrical activity → detect neural HL. ดังนั้น OAE alone ใน NICU → ไม่ยอมรับ (JCIH 2007). Zika-associated HL = auditory neuropathy pattern → ต้องใช้ AABR เช่นกัน. Well-baby nursery: OAE acceptable (ถ้า two-step → even better). Bottom line: ถ้า NICU = AABR, ถ้า WBN = OAE หรือ AABR.

PEARL 2 — 1-3-6 Rule: ทุกเดือนมีความหมาย
JCIH 1-3-6: screen ≤1 month → diagnostic ≤3 months → intervention ≤6 months. การพลาด deadline แต่ละจุด → outcome worse อย่างมีนัยสำคัญ. Yoshinaga-Itano (2003): intervention ก่อน 6 เดือน → language comparable to hearing peers at age 3. Vohr et al (2008): enrollment ≤3 months → significantly ↑ words understood + produced + gestures at 12–16 months. ใน NICU: diagnosis ก่อน discharge เป็น trend ที่ดี — maximizes time for intervention. อย่า delay เพราะ 'รอดูก่อน' — ทุกเดือนที่ brain ไม่ได้รับ auditory input = missed critical window.

PEARL 3 — GJB2 Connexin 26: Gene ที่ต้องตรวจ First-Line
GJB2 (connexin 26) = single most common gene; ≤50% ของ profound nonsyndromic HL; 35delG mutation = ≤70% ของ GJB2 mutations. Inheritance: AR. ดังนั้น parents มักได้ยินปกติ. >100 mutations identified. ทุก family ที่มี confirmed HL → ควร offer genetics evaluation + GJB2/GJB6 testing. Mitochondrial 12S rRNA mutation + aminoglycoside → ototoxicity — prevalence 1–1.8% ใน NICU แต่ HL มักเป็น variable-onset; awareness สำคัญ. GJB2 negative → ไม่ exclude genetic HL → full panel สำหรับ unexplained bilateral sensorineural HL.

PEARL 4 — Usher Syndrome: เลือก Communication Option ผิด → Disaster
Usher syndrome = most common cause of AR syndromic HL (4–6%); vestibular problems + progressive retinitis pigmentosa → deaf AND blind over time. ถ้าเลือก ASL as primary communication → เมื่อ vision ลดลง = ขาด communication mode ทั้งหมด. Strategy: cochlear implant early + auditory verbal หรือ auditory oral approach ก่อนที่ vision จะเสื่อม. Ophthalmology involvement early + genetic testing (USH1/USH2/USH3). Pearl: ทุก child with congenital HL → ≥1 ophthalmology exam; ถ้า Usher → urgency ↑.

PEARL 5 — Cochlear Implant Post-Meningitis: รีบ ก่อน Ossification
Bacterial meningitis → sensorineural HL; cochlear ossification (labyrinthitis ossificans) เริ่มได้ภายในไม่กี่เดือนหลัง meningitis → obstructs electrode insertion. ดังนั้นถ้า post-meningitis HL confirmed → cochlear implant evaluation urgently. Children with cochlear implants มีความเสี่ยง bacterial meningitis ↑ (Reefhuis NEJM 2003; Biernath Pediatrics 2006); Streptococcus pneumoniae most common. → Vaccinate ALL cochlear implant candidates per AAP high-risk pneumococcal schedule BEFORE implant. Monitor for all infections post-implant. Do not delay implant in bilateral profound HL post-meningitis.
9. Evidence Update — Key Studies

JCIH Position Statement Pediatrics 2007;120:898: 1-3-6 benchmark; NICU infants >5 days → AABR only; all infants with any degree unilateral/bilateral HL → early intervention ≤6 months; foundational UNHS guidelines

JCIH Year 2019 Position Statement J Early Hear Detect Interv 2019;4(2):1: updated risk factors (Box 61.1); expanded Zika virus as risk factor; updated cochlear implant candidacy; endorsed AABR in NICU

Yoshinaga-Itano C Ment Retard Dev Disabil Res Rev 2003;9:252: Colorado Newborn Hearing Screening Project — children with HL + intervention ≤6 months → language comparable to hearing peers at age 3; established the 6-month benchmark
Vohr B et al Pediatrics 2008;122:535: enrollment ≤3 months → significantly ↑ words understood, produced, gestures at 12–16 months; enrolled >3 months → significantly lower on all measures
Yoshinaga-Itano C et al Pediatrics 2020;146(suppl 3):S270: Colorado longitudinal: amount of child-directed parent talk first 4 years strongly associated with language outcomes at age 7 years

Watkin P, Baldwin M Int J Audiol 2012;51:519: universal neonatal hearing screening longitudinal follow-up: deafness prevalence 3.65/1,000 by school age vs 1.79/1,000 at birth — rate nearly doubles; highlights need for continued surveillance
Kimberlin DW et al NEJM 2015;372:933: valganciclovir 6 months vs 6 weeks for symptomatic congenital CMV — 6-month treatment → ↑ hearing and developmental outcomes at 24 months
Boppana SB et al J Pediatr 2005;146:817: congenital CMV — association between virus burden in infancy and HL; viral load predicts hearing outcome
Leal MC et al MMWR 2016;65(34):917: hearing loss in infants with microcephaly + congenital Zika — Brazil; HL type = sensorineural or auditory neuropathy; confirmed CNS + Zika → AABR screen
Reefhuis J et al NEJM 2003;349:435: risk of bacterial meningitis in children with cochlear implants; S. pneumoniae most common; ↑ risk vs general pediatric population → high-risk vaccination protocol
Leigh J et al Otol Neurotol 2013;34:443: cochlear implantation by 12 months → ↑ communication development outcomes; earlier implantation better even within first year of life
Gagnon EB et al J Speech Lang Hear Res 2021;64:1369: cumulative cochlear implant wear time → spoken language outcomes at age 3; greater wear time = better language regardless of implant age
Warner-Czyz AD et al Ear Hear 2022;43:268: American Cochlear Implant Alliance guidelines for determining cochlear implant candidacy in children; updated criteria including 9-month candidacy

Ealy M et al Laryngoscope 2011;121:1184: prevalence of mitochondrial mutations (12S rRNA) associated with aminoglycoside-induced HL in NICU population: 1.0% rate; no direct aminoglycoside + mutation → HL association demonstrated neonatally (variable onset)

Tomblin JB et al Ear Hear 2015;36(suppl 1):76S: language outcomes in young children with mild to severe HL — even mild HL negatively affects language; supports early identification + intervention for all degrees

Rosenfeld RM et al Otolaryngol HNS 2013;149(suppl 1):S1: clinical practice guideline for tympanostomy tubes; 3-month observation first; tubes for persistent OME ≥4 months with HL; no antihistamines/decongestants
Alford RL et al Genet Med 2014;16:347: ACMG guideline for clinical evaluation + etiologic diagnosis of HL; stepwise genetic testing approach; GJB2 first-tier; chromosomal/panel as needed

Vohr BR et al Arch Pediatr Adolesc Med 2008;162:205: family stress after UNHS — false-positive screen → ↑ worry 2–8 weeks; informed mothers → ↓ worry; false-positive → no lasting stress at 12–24 months; NICU stay contributes to prolonged stress
10. Self-Check Q&A
Q: ทำไม NICU infants >5 days ต้องใช้ AABR เท่านั้น ห้ามใช้ OAE?

ตอบ: Auditory neuropathy (neural HL): pathology อยู่ที่ IHC + CN VIII — outer hair cells intact → OAE passes แม้จะมี HL จริง. NICU infants เสี่ยง auditory neuropathy สูงสุด (prematurity, hyperbilirubinemia, HI). AABR detects wave 5 (CN VIII + brainstem activity) → identifies neural HL ได้. Well-baby nursery: OAE acceptable (neural HL less common). NICU: AABR only. Two-step AABR + OAE: acceptable in WBN, but AABR alone sufficient and preferred in NICU (JCIH 2007).
Q: อธิบาย 1-3-6 rule และ evidence ที่รองรับ?

ตอบ: 1 month: screen; 3 months: diagnostic confirmation by audiologist; 6 months: early intervention. Evidence: Yoshinaga-Itano (2003): intervention ≤6 months → language scores comparable to hearing peers at age 3. Vohr et al (2008): enrollment ≤3 months → ↑ words understood/produced/gestures at 12–16 months vs >3 months. Brain maximally responsive to language input early in life — missing the 6-month window = missed critical plasticity window (Cardon & Sharma 2012; Gilley et al 2010).
Q: GJB2 connexin 26 — ระบาดวิทยาและ clinical implication

ตอบ: GJB2: encodes connexin 26 (gap-junction protein in cochlea); ≤50% ของ all profound nonsyndromic HL; >100 mutations; 35delG = ≤70% ของ GJB2 mutations. Inheritance: AR → most infants born to hearing parents. ALL families with confirmed HL → genetics evaluation + GJB2/GJB6 testing. If GJB2 negative → full gene panel (ACMG guideline). Mitochondrial 12S rRNA: 1–1.8% NICU population; + aminoglycoside → aminoglycoside-induced HL risk; variable onset (may not manifest neonatally).
Q: ระบุ JCIH 2019 risk factors ที่เสี่ยงต่อ delayed-onset HL เป็นพิเศษ

ตอบ: Risk factors greater for delayed-onset/progressive HL (marked * in Box 61.1): (1) caregiver concern regarding hearing/speech/language; (2) family history of permanent HL; (4) in utero CMV* and Zika*; (7) syndromes (neurofibromatosis, Usher, Waardenburg)*; (8) neurodegenerative disorders*; (9) culture-positive postnatal infections (meningitis)*; (11) chemotherapy*. → These children need MORE frequent audiologic follow-up, not just one post-newborn assessment. HL rate doubles birth → school age (1.79 → 3.65/1,000) largely due to these delayed-onset cases.
Q: CMV กับ Zika — ผลต่อ hearing และ management ต่างกันอย่างไร?

ตอบ: CMV: most common medical cause of early AND delayed-onset HL; most asymptomatic at birth; HL type: sensorineural; valganciclovir 6 months for symptomatic CMV → ↑ hearing outcomes (Kimberlin NEJM 2015); controversial for asymptomatic CMV without HL. Zika: neurotropic; HL type = sensorineural OR auditory neuropathy; associated with CNS abnormalities → must use AABR (not OAE) (Leal MMWR 2016). Key difference: Zika → auditory neuropathy risk → AABR; CMV → predominantly sensorineural → OAE or AABR.
Q: Usher syndrome — ทำไมการเลือก communication option สำคัญมาก?

ตอบ: Usher = most common AR syndromic HL (4–6% HL): vestibular problems + progressive retinitis pigmentosa → blindness with increasing age. If ASL chosen as primary mode → when vision lost = communication method gone. Strategy: cochlear implant early + auditory verbal/oral approach → develops spoken language before vision deteriorates. All children with HL → ophthalmology exam ≥1 time. Usher → urgent ophthalmology + genetic subtyping (USH1 = severe HL + severe vestibular; USH2 = moderate-severe HL; USH3 = variable).
Q: อธิบาย tympanometry ใน infants — ทำไม standard probe ใช้ไม่ได้?

ตอบ: Standard tympanometry (226 Hz probe): ไม่แม่นยำใน infants <6 months เพราะ ear canal + tympanic membrane ยัง compliant มากกว่าในผู้ใหญ่ → ต้องใช้ high-frequency probe 1000 Hz ใน infants <6 months. Type A = normal mobility; flat curve = fluid in middle ear หรือ tympanic membrane perforation. Tympanometry ใช้สำหรับ diagnostic (ไม่ใช่ screening). สำคัญมากในเด็กที่มี permanent HL ซึ่ง superimposed OME → worse combined HL.
Q: OME ใน children with permanent HL — management approach

ตอบ: OME in permanent HL: mixed HL = worse combined hearing → monitor closely. Management: (1) 3-month observation first (75–90% spontaneous resolution); (2) NO antihistamines or decongestants (no evidence); (3) tympanostomy tubes for persistent OME ≥4 months + HL or structural injury (Rosenfeld et al 2013). Amplification adjustment may be needed during OME period. Balance problems with unilateral HL + OME (Engel-Yeger et al Disabil Rehabil 2004).
Q: Cochlear implant — candidacy criteria, timing, และ post-implant risks

ตอบ: Candidacy: bilateral severe-profound HL; limited benefit from hearing aids; appropriate medical/developmental status. Age: as young as 9 months (FDA 2020); bilateral profound HL before 12 months → best outcomes. Post-meningitis: implant urgently (cochlear ossification risk). Meningitis risk: S. pneumoniae most common pathogen (Reefhuis NEJM 2003) → vaccinate per AAP high-risk schedule before implant. Monitor all middle ear + systemic infections post-implant. Wear time critical: ↑ cumulative wear time → ↑ language at age 3 (Gagnon 2021).
Q: HL ที่ fail newborn screen — นอกจาก audiologist ต้องส่ง consult ใคร? ทำไม?

ตอบ: Multi-disciplinary team: (1) Otolaryngologist: etiology, amplification/surgical candidacy. (2) Geneticist: ALL families → GJB2/GJB6, gene panel, recurrence risk. (3) Ophthalmologist: ≥1 exam — HL association with vision impairment (Usher); vision essential for visual communication methods. (4) Developmental pediatrician/specialist: 30–40% of children with HL have comorbidities. (5) Early intervention provider: by 6 months at latest (JCIH). (6) Primary care: coordinate + support family; financial resources + support groups.

Day 159 Preview — Fanaroff Ch.62
บทต่อไป: Chapter 62 — Early Childhood Neurodevelopmental Outcomes of High-Risk Neonates. เตรียมตัว: neurodevelopmental outcomes ใน VLBW/ELBW — cognitive, language, motor, behavioral, socioemotional domains; Bayley Scales of Infant Development (BSID) — versions I/II/III/4th edition; NICU Network Neurobehavioral Scale (NNNS); predictors of adverse outcomes: brain MRI at term equivalent age, head growth, NICU morbidities; neuroprotective strategies: MgSO4, delayed cord clamping, skin-to-skin, NIDCAP, optimized nutrition; long-term follow-up program design.
